---
output: github_document
---

<!-- README.md is generated from README.Rmd. Please edit that file -->

```{r, echo = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>",
  fig.path = "man/figures/README-"
)
```

# `gtsummary_add` <img src='man/figures/sticker.png' align="right" width="200">


`gtsummary_add` is a package to modify gtsummary tables notably by including the number of non missing observations. 

background: gtsummary shows total Ns by default, which can lead to confusion, especially with regards the calculation of percentages in the presence of missing data. 


## Installation

`gtsummary_add` is easiest to install via 

<!-- ```{r, eval = FALSE} -->
<!-- install.packages('CTUtemplate', repos = c('https://dcr-unibe-ch.r-universe.dev', 'https://cloud.r-project.org')) -->
<!-- ``` -->

Linux users might have to install from source:

```{r, eval = FALSE}
remotes::install_github("dcr-unibe-ch/bltable")
```

This may require `Sys.setenv(R_REMOTES_NO_ERRORS_FROM_WARNINGS="true")` if packages were built under a different R version to the one you are using.

## Usage

```{r, message=FALSE}
library(gtsummary_add)
```


### Add code for observed Ns in gtsummary
gtsummary tables of class "tbl_summary" show total Ns by default, which can lead to confusion, especially with regards the calculation of percentages in the presence of missing data. 

For ungrouped datasets (i.e. no by argument in `tbl_summary`), using add_n is sufficient. For grouped datasets (`tbl_summary(by = something)`), additional columns with the observed Ns can be added, but it requires a few lines of code.

the function `add_nobs` in `gtsummary_add` allow to add the Nobs (i.e. number of non-missing data) to a table of class c('tbl_summary', 'gtsummary'), generated by `tbl_summary`.
Depending on the specified location Nobs will be:
  
  (i) added in a new column,
  (ii) displayed as statistics alongside the variable name
  (iii) added after the variable name within the same cell.

The 'Add Ns for gtsummary' addin places the necessary code into the active document. One part of the code is a function, which should be moved to an appropriate location for functions (e.g. 01_packages_functions.R). The rest can be integrated into a `tbl_summary` call.
